explanation: Highly permissive license with minimal restrictions on use, modification, and distribution.
Permissive license that also grants an explicit patent license, widely accepted in enterprise use.
Permissive license requiring only attribution and non-endorsement, safe for commercial use.
Simplified permissive license similar to BSD-3-Clause without the non-endorsement clause.
Functionally equivalent to MIT, permissive and safe for proprietary integration.
Permissive license with minimal restrictions, commonly used for compression and utility libraries.
File-level weak copyleft license; modified files must remain MPL-licensed but can be combined with proprietary code.
Weak copyleft license allowing linking from proprietary code, but modifications to the library itself must be shared.
Similar to LGPL-2.1 with additional patent and anti-tivoization clauses; safe for dynamic linking only.
Weak copyleft license requiring source disclosure of modifications when distributed.
Strong copyleft license requiring derivative works to be released under GPL, generally incompatible with proprietary software.
Strong copyleft license with additional patent and anti-tivoization terms, high legal risk if embedded in closed products.
Strongest copyleft license extending obligations to network use (SaaS), very high risk for proprietary products.
Usage governed by a third-party commercial license; requires legal review of terms before distribution.
No license metadata could be identified; usage rights are unverified and should be treated as high risk until confirmed.
